title: Wiedemann-Franz universality: predicted L0 vs measured metals
interpretation: Predicted universal Lorenz number L0 = 2.443e-8 W.Ohm.K^-2. Across 11 chemically diverse metals, measured L at 0C/100C has mean 2.511e-8 (std 0.229e-8). 20/22 measurements lie within 15% of L0. The clustering supports the universality claim (electrical and thermal transport share one partition structure, so their ratio is material-independent). The positive outliers (W, Mo) are the known transition-metal deviation from elastic-scattering WF and mark the regime where the universal ratio is only approximate -- reported, not hidden. Note: WF universality is standard physics; the framework REPRODUCES it, it does not predict a new value. Claim defensible only as 'the partition picture is consistent with WF', NOT as novel.
